title: Why Are We Here? Why Did We Raise $15B?
body: Andreessen Horowitz raised over $15B across multiple funds in 2025
Ben Horowitz frames the mission as providing opportunity regardless of background

title: Agenda: Our Mission and Stakes
body: Record-breaking fundraise across six investment strategies
America's technological leadership imperative
Competition with China and preservation of U.S. dominance
Human flourishing through technological advancement

title: Scale of Capital Deployment
body: Total raise: over $15B across multiple funds
Represents over 18% of all U.S. venture capital allocated in 2025
One of the largest venture capital raises in history

title: American Dynamism Fund
body: Fund size: $1.176B
Focused on strengthening American institutions and infrastructure
Part of broader strategy to maintain U.S. competitive advantage

title: Apps Fund
body: Fund size: $1.7B
Investing in next-generation application layer technologies
Consumer and enterprise software opportunities

title: Bio + Health Fund
body: Fund size: $700M
Applying technological advancement to biology and health
Critical sector for American leadership

title: Infrastructure Fund
body: Fund size: $1.7B
Building foundational technology platforms
Underpinning next wave of innovation

title: Growth Fund
body: Fund size: $6.75B
Largest single fund in the portfolio
Scaling proven companies to global dominance

title: Other Investment Strategies
body: Additional capital deployed: $3B
Diversified approach across emerging technology sectors
Flexible capital for strategic opportunities

title: Historical Foundation
body: America's system has produced unprecedented human flourishing over 250 years
Track record of creating opportunity regardless of background
Foundation for continued technological and economic leadership

title: Core Technology Focus Areas
body: The firm positions itself as responsible for American technological leadership in AI and crypto
AI and crypto applied to biology, health, defense, and education
Cross-sector technology deployment strategy

title: Strategic Imperative: China Competition
body: Competition with China intensifies across all domains
Warning that losing technological dominance risks economic, military, geopolitical, and cultural leadership
Technology leadership as existential national priority

title: Mission: Opportunity for All
body: Ben Horowitz frames the mission as providing opportunity regardless of background
Democratizing access to technological advancement and wealth creation
Continuing America's tradition of meritocratic advancement

title: What's at Stake
body: Economic leadership depends on maintaining technology edge
Military superiority tied to technological advancement
Geopolitical and cultural influence flows from innovation leadership
The firm sees itself as steward of American competitiveness

title: Outlook: The Responsibility Ahead
body: $15B+ deployment represents commitment to American technological future
Multi-sector strategy addresses comprehensive national competitiveness
Mission-driven approach to venture capital in era of great power competition
